parts.title: Central-Level Strategists
prompts: Create a strategic plan for a school strategist at a principal authority level to raise the quality of [specific area, e.g. mathematics teaching] across all schools, with three steps and a three-year timeframe.
Give me five strategies for a central-level school strategist to promote [specific goal, e.g. equity] across the organisation, with one practical action for each and a link to the Education Act.

Write a short guide for a school strategist at a principal authority level to coordinate a [specific professional development initiative, e.g. digital competence] for teachers across all school units, with three steps and a follow-up plan.

Suggest three ways for a central-level school strategist to implement [specific initiative, e.g. source criticism teaching] across multiple schools, with one strategy for each and a link to [reference to current curriculum].

Create a list of five concise strategies for a school strategist at a principal authority level to strengthen [specific focus, e.g. student health] across the organisation, with one action for each and a rationale.
Give me an example of a routine for a central-level school strategist to monitor [specific goal, e.g. equitable assessment] across all schools, with three steps and a plan for reporting to the board.

Suggest three ways for a school strategist at a principal authority level to collaborate with school leaders to prioritise [specific project, e.g. digitalisation], with one strategy for each and a timeline.
Create a plan for a central-level school strategist to develop a [specific policy, e.g. anti-bullying policy] for all schools, with three steps and an implementation strategy.

Give me five ideas for a school strategist at a principal authority level to streamline [specific process, e.g. resource allocation] across the organisation, with one solution for each and an efficiency gain.
Write a short template for a central-level school strategist to use when planning a [specific change, e.g. increased digital competence], with three steps and a communication plan for school leaders.

Suggest three ways for a school strategist at a principal authority level to coordinate support for teachers in using [specific pedagogical tool, e.g. formative assessment tools] across all schools, with one idea for each.

Give me an example of how a central-level school strategist can initiate a [specific project, e.g. inclusion of newly arrived students] across the organisation, with three steps and an evaluation plan.

Create a list of five strategic focus areas for a school strategist at a principal authority level to improve [specific goal, e.g. student outcomes in mathematics], with a short description and benefit for each.
Suggest three ways for a central-level school strategist to address [specific challenge, e.g. teacher shortage] across the organisation, with one strategy for each and a rationale.

Write a short guide for a school strategist at a principal authority level to introduce a [specific routine, e.g. systematic quality assurance] for all schools, with three steps and a link to the Education Act.

Give me five ideas for a central-level school strategist to strengthen [specific focus, e.g. digitalisation] across the organisation, with one action for each and a link to [reference to current curriculum].
Suggest three ways for a school strategist at a principal authority level to collaborate with external partners to develop [specific project, e.g. technology integration], with one strategy for each.
Create a plan for a central-level school strategist to carry out a [specific evaluation, e.g. of student outcomes] across all schools, with three steps and an analysis strategy.
Give me an example of how a school strategist at a principal authority level can introduce [specific initiative, e.g. anti-bullying] for all schools, with three steps and a follow-up plan.
Write a list of three concise ways for a central-level school strategist to use [specific tool, e.g. analytics tool] to monitor [specific goal, e.g. equity], with one suggestion for each.
Create a strategy for a school strategist at a principal authority level to improve [specific focus, e.g. student health] across all schools, with three steps and a plan for measuring results.
Give me five ways for a central-level school strategist to streamline [specific process, e.g. teacher recruitment] across the organisation, with one solution for each and an efficiency gain.

Write a short guide for a school strategist at a principal authority level to coordinate a [specific professional development initiative, e.g. inclusion] for all staff, with three steps and a follow-up plan.

Suggest three ways for a central-level school strategist to introduce [specific initiative, e.g. digital tools] for all schools, with one strategy for each and a link to [reference to current curriculum].

Create a list of five concise strategies for a school strategist at a principal authority level to address [specific challenge, e.g. declining student attendance] across the organisation, with one action for each.
Give me an example of a routine for a central-level school strategist to monitor [specific goal, e.g. student outcomes] across all schools, with three steps and a plan for reporting to the board.

Suggest three ways for a school strategist at a principal authority level to collaborate with teacher unions to carry out [specific project, e.g. work environment improvement], with one strategy for each.
Write a short template for a central-level school strategist to use when planning a [specific change, e.g. increased digitalisation], with three steps and a communication plan for school leaders.
Give me five ideas for a school strategist at a principal authority level to strengthen [specific focus, e.g. equity] across the organisation, with one solution for each and an efficiency gain.
Create a plan for a central-level school strategist to develop a [specific routine, e.g. crisis management] for all schools, with three steps and an implementation strategy.
Suggest three ways for a school strategist at a principal authority level to coordinate [specific project, e.g. AI use in teaching] with external partners, with one strategy for each and a rationale.
Give me an example of how a central-level school strategist can introduce [specific initiative, e.g. student influence] for all schools, with three steps and an evaluation plan.
Write a list of five tools for a school strategist at a principal authority level to use for [specific purpose, e.g. data analysis of student outcomes], with a description and benefit for each.
Suggest three ways for a central-level school strategist to streamline [specific process, e.g. resource allocation] across the organisation, with one solution for each and an efficiency gain.
Create a plan for a central-level school strategist to carry out a [specific evaluation, e.g. of digital competence] for all schools, with three steps and an analysis strategy.

Give me five ideas for a school strategist at a principal authority level to address [specific situation, e.g. declining student outcomes] across the organisation, with one action for each and a follow-up plan.
Suggest three ways for a central-level school strategist to collaborate with the IT department to introduce [specific project, e.g. new digital devices], with one strategy for each.
Write a short guide for a school strategist at a principal authority level to introduce a [specific routine, e.g. anti-bullying] for all schools, with three steps and a staff support plan.
Give me an example of how a central-level school strategist can use [specific tool, e.g. analytics tool] to monitor [specific goal, e.g. student health], with three steps and a rationale.

Create a list of three concise ways for a central-level school strategist to use [specific tool, e.g. digital surveys] to strengthen [specific goal, e.g. student influence], with one suggestion for each and a link to [reference to current curriculum].
